close_reason: Added loop to reject unknown query parameters in from_uri
description:
Trigger: user constructs URI like 'secretx:desktop:my-app/api-key?ttl=300' or 'secretx:desktop:my-app/api-key?fieldname=password' (typo of field=).
Behavior: lib.rs:83 only rejects param('field'). Any other parameter is silently ignored. The user thinks their parameter took effect.

Compare: same pattern in secretx-keyring/src/lib.rs:94 — workspace-wide convention or workspace-wide oversight?

Fix options:
1. Reject any unknown parameter. Loud failure on typos. Risk: callers passing well-known params expected by other backends (e.g. ?cache=...) get rejected for desktop.
2. Whitelist of accepted params (currently empty for desktop) and reject everything else. Forward-compatible.
3. Accept and log a warning. Doesn't help test-only or production paths without logs hooked up.

Recommend option 2 as a workspace-level decision; out of scope for a single-crate fix unless the convention is set first. File the issue, defer.

Note: this is partly an opinion-grade design call. The 'security-first' framing argues for option 2; the 'flexibility' framing argues for current behavior. Mark this finding for triage rather than auto-fix.
title: Unknown query parameters silently accepted; only ?field= is rejected
